profile.geographical_reach: global — operations in 30+ countries; strong in infrastructure-rich markets across Americas, Europe, Middle East, Asia-Pacific, and Australia; 100% AM economics internalized from BN in 2025
profile.business_focus: asset_management — pure-play fee-bearing AM business; completed full internalization of 100% AM economics from Brookfield Corporation in 2025. New CEO Connor Teskey effective 2026. Fee-Bearing Capital $603B (+12% YoY; 87% long-dated or perpetual). FRE $3B (+22% YoY), 58% margin.
profile.asset_focus: large_caps — predominantly large-cap infrastructure and real asset transactions; mid-market through Oaktree credit strategies

asset_classes_strategies.PE: Corporate PE buyout through Brookfield Capital Partners series; Oaktree manages ~$200B across distressed, growth equity, and credit-oriented PE.
asset_classes_strategies.Credit: Oaktree Capital Management (acquired 2019) provides credit platform spanning distressed debt, high yield, leveraged loans, CLOs, and performing credit. Integrated with insurance channel (Brookfield Wealth Solutions).
asset_classes_strategies.Infrastructure: Flagship platform — largest infrastructure investor globally. BIP, Super-Core Infrastructure, transition/renewables infrastructure. $250B+ in real assets AUM. Megatrend focus on energy transition, digital infrastructure, AI data centers.
asset_classes_strategies.Real_Estate: BREP series, core real estate, logistics, mixed-use, and transitional strategies. Significant global portfolio across Americas, Europe, Asia.
asset_classes_strategies.Natural_Resources: Renewable power and transition (BGTF series, BEP), timber, agriculture. Climate transition is a core strategic pillar with $100B+ committed.

profile.sector_orientation: sector_focused — strong thematic concentrations in infrastructure, renewables/transition, real estate, and credit; operating heritage in real assets provides origination advantage
profile.origination_engine: captive_origination — proprietary origination through operating platforms in infrastructure, renewables, and real estate; Brookfield's operator DNA provides unique deal flow access
profile.fund_type: drawdown, evergreen, listed — traditional drawdown partnerships, perpetual/listed vehicles (BIP, BEP, BBU), and growing open-end structures. 87% of fee-bearing capital is long-dated or perpetual.
profile.capital_source: non_captive_capital_pool and captive_capital_pool — third-party fee-bearing capital $603B (+12% YoY); Brookfield Wealth Solutions insurance channel; affiliated capital from BN ecosystem
profile.distribution_strategy: direct, consultants, distributors — institutional direct, consultant channels, expanding private wealth through Brookfield Oaktree Wealth Solutions. Raised >$110B in 2025.
profile.client_segment: pension_funds, swf, insurance, endowments_foundations, family_offices, private_banks, hnwi — large institutional base; insurance growing via Wealth Solutions; private wealth expanding
profile.growth_agenda: organic and inorganic — organic through flagship fund vintages, perpetual capital scaling, and transition/infrastructure megatrends; inorganic through AEI Net Lease (2024), full BAM internalization (2025). FRE target to double in five years.
profile.share_class: single_class — Class A limited voting shares on NYSE/TSX since Dec 2022 spin-off
stated_strategic_priorities: Double fee-related earnings over five years by scaling flagship funds and adjacent strategies; record FRE $3.0B in 2025 (+22% YoY), 58% FRE margin; raised >$110B in 2025
Capture $100T+ energy transition and infrastructure investment opportunity as the dominant global real assets platform; committed $100B+ to transition capital globally

Grow insurance (Brookfield Wealth Solutions) and private wealth channels as permanent capital accelerators; 15% dividend increase; new CEO Connor Teskey (2026) intensifies AM focus post-internalization

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Infrastructure investment gap estimated at $3.5T/year globally (McKinsey). Renewable/transition capital needs $3-5T/year by 2030. BAM uniquely positioned at intersection of real assets and transition capital.

contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: SEC-registered. Subject to AIFMD II in Europe (effective Jan 2026). Insurance regulation through Brookfield Wealth Solutions. Canadian securities regulation for TSX listing.

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: European PE market ~$800B AUM. CVC's EUR 148B FPAUM positions it as one of Europe's largest alt managers. IPO proceeds provide balance sheet optionality for acquisitions and co-investment.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Amsterdam listing completed April 2024 at EUR 14/share. Pre-IPO partners retain significant equity and influence. Single share class. CEO Rob Lucas. Largest European alt manager listing in a decade.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: Luxembourg-regulated AIFM. EU AIFMD II (effective Jan 2026). Dutch public company governance code (AFM). Multiple regulatory jurisdictions across 31 offices.

profile.geographical_reach: global — headquartered in Bala Cynwyd, PA; 20+ offices across Americas, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Middle East
profile.business_focus: asset_management — pure-play private markets firm: discretionary fund management ($146.1B AUM) and advisory/non-discretionary ($871.5B). Total AUM and supervision $1.0T. Equity Advantage evergreen platform expanding. Cobalt data platform differentiates advisory.
profile.asset_focus: mid_market — SMA and solutions spans all segments; direct fund investments target mid-market PE, credit, infrastructure co-investments

asset_classes_strategies.PE: Buyout, growth, secondaries, co-investments, primaries. Broadest PE coverage through SMA and fund-of-funds. Direct co-investment fund series.
asset_classes_strategies.Credit: Direct lending, mezzanine, special situations, primaries, secondaries. Growing dedicated credit capability via specialized funds.
asset_classes_strategies.Infrastructure: Core-plus, value-added primaries, secondaries, co-investments. Growing dedicated infrastructure fund capability.
asset_classes_strategies.Real_Estate: PE-style and core-plus real estate through primaries, secondaries, co-investments.
asset_classes_strategies.missing_reason_Natural_Resources: some natural resources through infrastructure allocations

profile.sector_orientation: sector_agnostic — solutions/advisory spans all sectors; Cobalt data platform covers 50,000+ private markets funds
profile.origination_engine: primary_originator — proprietary deal flow through advisory relationships; Cobalt LP portfolio monitoring as sourcing and analytics engine; $1T+ capital responsibility
profile.fund_type: sma, drawdown, evergreen — SMAs core; commingled and evergreen vehicles (Private Assets Fund, Equity Advantage); interval funds for wealth channel
profile.distribution_strategy: direct, consultants, distributors — institutional direct; consultant; growing wealth via wirehouses (Morgan Stanley, UBS, Merrill), RIAs, and Equity Advantage platform
profile.client_segment: pension_funds, swf, endowments_foundations, insurance, corporate, family_offices, hnwi, retail — broad institutional; expanding rapidly into wealth channel via Equity Advantage
profile.growth_agenda: organic — primarily through SMA mandates, evergreen vehicle scaling, co-investment direct deployment. $146.1B discretionary AUM. Q3 FY26 EPS $1.55 (beat estimate).
profile.share_class: dual_class — Class A (public, 1 vote) and Class B (Hamilton Lane Advisors LLC, 10 votes). IPO March 2017.
stated_strategic_priorities: Scale customized SMA platform as core client-acquisition engine; $871.5B in advisory/non-discretionary; Cobalt data platform across 50,000+ funds is defensible moat

Expand evergreen and semi-liquid via Equity Advantage (direct co-investment perpetual vehicle) and Private Assets Fund for wealth-channel democratization; Morgan Stanley, UBS, Merrill wirehouse distribution
Leverage proprietary Cobalt data and technology platform to differentiate advisory and drive operational alpha; technology revenue growing as distinct line item

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Hamilton Lane operates across ~$14T global private markets allocation opportunity. Total capital under management and supervision ~$1T provides data advantage difficult to replicate.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Dual-class structure with Class B concentrating voting control. IPO March 2017. Erik Hirsch became CEO in 2024.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: SEC-registered. Evergreen and interval fund products regulated under Investment Company Act. Global operations require FCA, BaFin, MAS, and other local compliance.

profile.geographical_reach: regional — headquartered in Sao Paulo/Grand Cayman; operations across Brazil, Chile, Colombia, and selectively US (GP minority stakes). LatAm-focused with nascent developed-market exposure.
profile.business_focus: asset_management — pure-play LatAm-focused alt manager with six segments: PE, Infrastructure, Credit, Real Estate, Public Equities, and GP Minority Stakes (GPMS). $52.6B AUM (+26% YoY). FEAUM $40.8B (+24% YoY). Pro-forma FEAUM $47.4B incl. announced acquisitions. FRE $202.5M (+19%).
profile.asset_focus: mid_market — LatAm mid-market across PE, infrastructure, credit, real estate; GP stakes target US mid-market GPs

asset_classes_strategies.PE: Buyout (Fund VII series), growth equity (iGah3 acquisition 2024), co-investments. LatAm-focused mid-market PE. ~$10B PE AUM.
asset_classes_strategies.Credit: Direct lending, special situations, asset-backed across Brazil, Chile, Colombia. Growing from subscale to ~$5B+ FEAUM. Multiple fund vintages 2022-2025.
asset_classes_strategies.Infrastructure: Value-added, core-plus infrastructure across LatAm: energy, transport, logistics, telecom. Flagship strategy. ~$15B infra AUM.
asset_classes_strategies.Real_Estate: PE-style and core-plus real estate in Brazil: logistics, commercial, residential. ~$5B RE AUM.
asset_classes_strategies.missing_reason_Natural_Resources: no standalone strategy; some climate/energy transition through infrastructure
asset_classes_strategies.GP_Minority_Stakes: US GP minority stakes (GPMS): non-controlling positions in mid-sized US alt managers. ~$6-7B pro-forma FEAUM. Revenue diversification beyond LatAm.

profile.sector_orientation: sector_agnostic — LatAm generalist across sectors; infrastructure has sector focus (energy, transport, digital); GPMS is sector-diversified
profile.origination_engine: captive_origination — proprietary LatAm deal sourcing through local office network; 30+ year LatAm relationships; GPMS leverages US GP network
profile.fund_type: drawdown, listed — traditional drawdown PE, credit, RE funds; listed infrastructure vehicles (some Brazilian-listed FIPs); no evergreen/semi-liquid structures yet
profile.capital_source: non_captive_capital_pool — third-party institutional; growing DFI and international investor allocation to LatAm alternatives
profile.distribution_strategy: direct, consultants, placement_agents — institutional direct; consultant-intermediated; placement agent-assisted for international LP fundraising
profile.client_segment: pension_funds, swf, dfis, family_offices, hnwi — LatAm pension funds as anchor; DFI (IFC, IDB) relationships; international institutional allocators; limited retail
profile.growth_agenda: inorganic — acquisition-driven: iGah3 (growth equity, 2024), Moneda (Chile, 2022), multiple announced acquisitions driving pro-forma FEAUM to $47.4B. Record $7.7B organic fundraising in 2025. 2026 target $7B.
profile.share_class: dual_class — Class A (public) and Class B (super-voting, founders/senior partners). Cayman Islands incorporated; 2021 NASDAQ IPO.
stated_strategic_priorities: Expand FEAUM toward $50B+ through bolt-on acquisitions and organic fund launches across LatAm and selectively developed markets; pro-forma FEAUM $47.4B incl. announced acquisitions; 2026 fundraising target $7B
Diversify beyond core LatAm franchise through US GP minority stakes (GPMS, ~$6-7B FEAUM) and growth equity (iGah3); revenue diversification is explicit strategic objective
Scale private credit and infrastructure platforms to capture LatAm institutional under-allocation to alternatives; FRE $202.5M (+19%); record organic fundraising $7.7B in 2025

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: LatAm alternative AUM estimated at $150-200B, with Brazil comprising ~60%. Pension fund allocation to alternatives remains below OECD averages. Structural tailwind for domestic managers with local origination and relationships.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Dual-class share structure. Cayman Islands holding company. CEO Alex Saigh. NASDAQ-listed since 2021. CFO transition to Raphael Denadai effective April 2026.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: CVM (Brazil) regulated for fund management. Chilean (CMF), Colombian (SFC), and other LatAm securities regulation. US GPMS introduces SEC/state-level considerations.

profile.geographical_reach: global — headquartered in Paris; offices across Europe (Paris, London, Luxembourg, Berlin, Madrid), US (New York), and Asia (Shanghai, Singapore, Seoul). European PE heritage with growing international distribution.
profile.business_focus: asset_management — majority-alt manager with hybrid model: third-party fund management plus significant proprietary balance sheet (~EUR 9.3B). EUR 36.8B AUM at H1 2025 (+4% YoY). Third-party AUM EUR 27.5B (+10% YoY). Full-year 2025 results not yet published.
profile.asset_focus: mid_market — European mid-market PE specialist; growth equity; private credit; real assets

asset_classes_strategies.PE: European mid-market buyout (Eurazeo Capital, Eurazeo PME), growth equity. Consumer, healthcare, technology, business services. EUR 12B+ PE AUM.
asset_classes_strategies.Credit: Eurazeo Private Debt: direct lending, structured credit, healthcare lending. EUR 5-6B credit AUM. Fastest-growing third-party segment.
asset_classes_strategies.missing_reason_Infrastructure: no dedicated infrastructure fund; real assets through PE portfolio and balance sheet
asset_classes_strategies.Real_Estate: Real estate through balance sheet and co-investment vehicles. European commercial and residential.
asset_classes_strategies.Venture_and_Growth: Venture and growth equity through dedicated teams: mid-cap growth, venture, China growth. EUR 4B+ AUM.

profile.sector_orientation: sector_focused — concentrated in healthcare, technology/software, consumer brands, financial services, business services
profile.origination_engine: captive_origination — European office network and sector teams; balance sheet co-investment provides deal credibility
profile.fund_type: drawdown, listed — traditional drawdown PE and credit funds; listed on Euronext Paris; balance sheet provides permanent capital component
profile.capital_source: proprietary_capital and non_captive_capital_pool — hybrid: ~EUR 9.3B proprietary (~25% of total AUM) alongside EUR 27.5B third-party
profile.distribution_strategy: direct, consultants, distributors — institutional direct; consultant-intermediated; growing Asian distribution (Korea, Japan, Singapore); European private bank partnerships
profile.client_segment: pension_funds, insurance, swf, family_offices, endowments_foundations, private_banks, hnwi — European institutional; growing Asian and Middle Eastern allocators
profile.growth_agenda: organic and inorganic — organic through fund vintages and product expansion; historically acquisitive (iM Global Partner stake, Rhone Capital, Kurma). Third-party AUM EUR 27.5B (+10% YoY).
profile.share_class: single_class — Euronext Paris (SBF 120); single class of ordinary shares; broadly dispersed ownership
stated_strategic_priorities: Accelerate third-party AUM toward EUR 40B by scaling credit, growth equity, and mid-market PE; third-party AUM EUR 27.5B (+10% YoY); shift AM contribution in earnings mix
Optimize balance sheet portfolio to maximize alignment value while selectively divesting mature positions to recycle capital into next generation of fund strategies
Expand international distribution with focus on Asian institutional investors (Korea, Japan, Singapore); deepen European wealth-channel penetration through private bank partnerships

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: European mid-market PE ~$200B addressable. French PE market second-largest in Europe. Eurazeo's EUR 36.8B AUM is mid-scale, comparable to Antin and Bridgepoint.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Euronext Paris listed. CEO Christophe Baviere since 2024. Historical Lazard and David-Weill family heritage. Single share class with no controlling shareholder — unusual for French AM firms.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: AMF (France) regulated. EU AIFMD-compliant. Luxembourg fund domicile. SFDR for sustainability-labeled funds. AIFMD II effective Jan 2026.

profile.geographical_reach: regional — headquartered in Paris; offices in London, New York, Singapore, and Luxembourg. Primarily European with expanding North American and Asian presence. Investment activity concentrated in Europe and North America.
profile.business_focus: asset_management — pure-play infrastructure specialist. EUR 33B+ AUM (H1 2025). Fee-Paying AUM EUR 21.8B (+6.2% YoY). Q3 2025 activity update confirms good momentum. One of the few publicly listed managers focused exclusively on infrastructure. Revenue driven by long-duration infrastructure fund management fees.
profile.asset_focus: mid_market and large_caps — mid- and large-cap infrastructure equity and debt; flagship targets EUR 2-5B enterprise value; mid-cap (NextGen) targets EUR 200M-1B

asset_classes_strategies.missing_reason_PE: no traditional PE buyout; exclusively infrastructure
asset_classes_strategies.Credit: Infrastructure debt through NextGen Fund series: senior and subordinated infrastructure lending. Complementary to equity franchise.
asset_classes_strategies.Infrastructure: Core franchise — mid-cap (Antin Infrastructure Partners Fund V), flagship (raising Fund VI, targeting EUR 12-15B), and NextGen (infrastructure debt). Energy transition, digital infrastructure, transport, social, environment/circular economy. EUR 33B+ total AUM.
asset_classes_strategies.missing_reason_Real_Estate: no dedicated real estate; some real assets through infrastructure portfolio
asset_classes_strategies.missing_reason_Natural_Resources: energy transition embedded within core infrastructure

profile.sector_orientation: sector_specific — exclusively infrastructure across five verticals: energy transition, digital, transport, social, environment/circular economy
profile.origination_engine: captive_origination — sector-specialist teams in each vertical; long-established European infrastructure relationships with utilities, owners, and government concession authorities
profile.fund_type: drawdown — predominantly traditional drawdown with 10-15 year fund lives; infrastructure debt (NextGen) as complementary strategy; exploring semi-liquid for future wealth access
profile.capital_source: non_captive_capital_pool — third-party institutional; no captive insurance or balance sheet co-investment
profile.distribution_strategy: direct, consultants — institutional direct; consultant-intermediated; limited wealth-channel; exploring semi-liquid structures for future wealth access
profile.client_segment: pension_funds, swf, insurance, endowments_foundations, family_offices — European pension and insurance heavy; growing North American and Asian SWF allocators; infrastructure-dedicated mandate allocators
profile.growth_agenda: organic — fund vintage succession (Fund V, Fund VI raising); NextGen infrastructure debt as product extension; no significant M&A history
profile.share_class: single_class — Euronext Paris listed since September 2021; single class of ordinary shares
stated_strategic_priorities: Raise Fund VI at target EUR 12-15B to cement position as one of Europe's leading mid-to-large-cap infrastructure platforms; Fund V progression demonstrates LP appetite for pure-play infra exposure
Scale NextGen infrastructure debt to EUR 5B+ AUM as complementary product providing senior/subordinated lending alongside equity co-investors

Expand geographic reach in North America (New York office) and Asia-Pacific (Singapore) while maintaining core European infrastructure origination edge; explore semi-liquid products for wealth channel

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Global infrastructure investment gap $3.5T/year (McKinsey). European infrastructure PE AUM ~$200B+. Antin EUR 33B+ AUM makes it one of largest listed pure-play infrastructure specialists.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Euronext Paris listed since September 2021. Co-founders Alain Rauscher and Mark Crosbie retain significant equity and operational leadership. Single share class.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: AMF (France) regulated. EU AIFMD-compliant. Luxembourg fund domicile. SFDR for sustainability-labeled funds. Infrastructure investments subject to sectoral regulation across European jurisdictions.
